contextual_role: Benchmark evidence for context/experience reuse across related repository-level coding tasks.
full_text_rationale: Full text describes SWE-ContextBench as a benchmark explicitly evaluating programming agents' ability to accumulate, retrieve, and reuse prior context/experience across related coding tasks.
extractor_notes: Contextual benchmark; includes memory/retrieval variants but main contribution is benchmark and measurement.
autores: Jared Zhu, Minhao Hu, Junde Wu
titulo: SWE Context Bench: A Benchmark for Context Learning in Coding
formato_armazenamento: Experience pool of prior coding-task trajectories or compact summaries retrieved for related tasks.
framework_agente: Claude Code based programming agent plus memory/retrieval variants
benchmarks_utilizados: SWE-ContextBench: 1,100 base tasks and 376 related tasks from 51 repositories and 9 languages; SWE-ContextBench Lite: 300 base and 99 related Python tasks.
metricas_desempenho: Patch applicability, FAIL_TO_PASS tests/tasks, PASS_TO_PASS tests/tasks, resolved rate, runtime, estimated API cost.
metricas_cl: Context reuse accuracy, time efficiency, and cost efficiency across related coding tasks.
baseline_comparado: No-Experience baseline, Free Experience Reuse, Oracle Experience Reuse, Free Summary Reuse, Oracle Summary Reuse, Supermemory, OpenViking, mem0.
custo_por_tarefa: No-Experience $0.79; Oracle Experience $0.77; Oracle Summary $0.85; Supermemory $0.58; OpenViking $0.53; mem0 $0.62 on 99 Lite related tasks.
reducao_tokens: NR exact token reduction; reports cost and runtime differences.
resultado_principal: SWE-ContextBench explicitly measures whether programming agents can accumulate, retrieve, and reuse prior context. Oracle Summary Reuse improves resolution to 34.34% versus 26.26% baseline; Supermemory reaches 30.30% resolved and OpenViking 29.20% with lower runtime/cost.
desempenho_vs_baseline: Oracle Summary Reuse: 34.34% resolved vs 26.26% No-Experience (+8.08pp). Supermemory: 30.30%; OpenViking: 29.20%; mem0: 24.24%.
custo_vs_baseline: OpenViking reduces average runtime/cost to 4.20 min/$0.53 vs No-Experience 6.37 min/$0.79; Supermemory 5.04 min/$0.58.
limitacoes: Lite experiments focus on 99 related Python tasks; autonomous context selection can be noisy; benchmark is preprint-stage.
ameacas_validade: Related-task construction, dependency setup, retrieval quality, and use of one Claude Code-based agent may affect external validity.
